name: Andrej Karpathy
role: Former Tesla AI Director; OpenAI founding team
summary: Warned that the LiteLLM PyPI supply-chain compromise showed how one poisoned dependency can exfiltrate broad credentials, and argued teams should reduce dependency sprawl where possible.

name: Guillermo Rauch
summary: Said internally generated apps/agent interfaces are replacing many SaaS workflows, and announced elastic build machines to optimize CPU-heavy Rust build performance for speed/cost.

name: Guillermo Rauch
summary: Announced intelligent hardware selection for builds, arguing modern compilers scale with CPU count so infra should adapt dynamically for agent-era workloads.

name: Aaron Levie
summary: Argued that software must be consumable headlessly via APIs for agents; otherwise platforms risk irrelevance as agent usage outscales direct human usage.

name: Claude
summary: Introduced Claude Code auto mode with per-tool-call risk checks to automate safe actions while blocking/rerouting risky operations.

name: Sam Altman
summary: Announced OpenAI Foundation and at least $1B over the next year focused on AI resilience, including scientific upside and societal risk response.

name: Peter Yang
summary: Advocated for AI agents reducing process overhead so product teams can focus on end-user iteration loops instead of heavy documentation workflows.

name: Nan Yu
summary: Said personal output and quality bar increased while manually writing PRDs/issues/code dropped, reinforcing agent-augmented product execution.

name: Garry Tan
summary: Framed AI as leverage expansion rather than developer replacement, comparing it to transportation that expands reachable outcomes.

name: Nikunj Kothari
summary: Launched DeployGraph by scraping 417 subprocessor lists to map which infra stacks AI-native companies are actually using.

name: Zara Zhang
summary: Positioned code as self-expression and highlighted rapid creator momentum through AI-assisted shipping and distribution workflows.

name: Aditya Agarwal
role: General Partner @ South Park Commons; ex-CTO @ Dropbox
summary: Argued robotics hardware is increasingly ready, shifting the bottleneck to reliable, scalable software systems.

name: Heather Kulik
summary: Explained that AI can accelerate materials discovery via active learning and multi-objective optimization, but robust progress depends on higher-fidelity data and stronger validation against real experiments.
